title: digitalis purpurea var. purpurea
intro: Digitalis purpurea var. purpurea, also known as purple foxglove, is a variety of the flowering plant Digitalis purpurea, native to Europe and western Asia. This biennial plant is a medicinal herb that has been used for centuries to treat heart conditions, particularly congestive heart failure and atrial fibrillation, due to its unique compound digoxin. The medicinal properties of Digitalis purpurea var. purpurea include its ability to increase the force of the heartbeat and slow heart rate, which can help to reduce symptoms of heart failure. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried extract, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote heart health and alleviate symptoms of heart conditions.
